LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. AND ROBERT OZAKI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND INCONVENIENT FORUM ARE GROUNDS FOR SEPARATE MOTIONS, NOT A BASIS FOR A DEMURRRER. CCP 418.10(A). IN ANY EVENT, THEY WERE NOT RAISED AT THE TIME OF THE FILING OF THE FIRST DEMURRER AND ARE WAIVED. CCP 418.10(E)(3). FURTHER, THE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE IS NOT A BASIS TO SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS OF FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER CCP 430.10(E). CCP 410.40, CITED BY PLAINTIFF, IS NOT A THRESHOLD REQUIREMENT AND IS INAPPLICABLE HERE. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CLAIM AND DELIVERY IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN AND IS NOT SUBJECT TO HEIGHTENED PLEADING REQUIREMENTS. THE DEMURRER FOR LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION IS OVERRULED AS DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED NO AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT THAT CALIFORNIA COURTS HAVE NO JURISDICTION OVER CONTRACTS SUCH AS THE ONE AT ISSUE. THIS IS DEFENDANT'S SECOND DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT; DEFENDANT IS NOW DIRECTED TO FILE AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT WITHIN TEN DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BODY DETAILS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS AGREED THAT SAN FRANCISCO IS THE APPROPRIATE FORUM FOR LITIGATING ANY DISPUTES ARISING OUT OF THE LEASE. THE COURT HAS SUBJECT MATTER JURISIDICTION. PACIFIC DECISION SCIENCES CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 121 CAL.APP.4TH 1100 IS NOT ON POINT. PACIFIC DECISION HELD THAT A COURT EXCEEDS ITS JURISDICTION WHEN IT ISSUES A TURNOVER ORDER FOR PROPERTY OUTSIDE THE STATE. THE HOLDING IN THAT CASE WAS BASED ON STRICT CONSTRUCTION OF LAW PERTAINING TO ATTACHMENT, AND IS NOT APPLICABLE TO WHETHER THE COURT IN THIS CASE HAS SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DECLARATION OF ROBERT OZAKI IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS RESPONSE IN OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 54946452) FILED BY DEFENDANT BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION OZAKI, ROBERT
REPLY DECLARATION OF MARC T. CEFALU IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES FILED BY PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS UNCONTESTED. DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION AND ROBERT OZAKI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IS DENIED. THE MOTION DOES NOT CONFORM TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1324, WHICH PROVIDES THAT A DECLARATION MUST ACCOMPANY THE MOTION. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT SUBMITS SUCH A DECLARATION IN REPLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT IT WOULD BE UNDULY PREJUDICIAL TO PLAINTIFF TO CONSIDER THIS EVIDENCE, GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MET ITS BURDEN IN ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT BODY DETAILS ENTERED INTO TWO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENTS WITH PLAINTIFF, (UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT ("UF" NOS. 1, 10), DEFENDANT ROBERT HATSUO OZAKI EXECUTED GUARANTEES ON THE AGREEMENTS (UF NOS. 5, 14), PLAINTIFFS PERFORMED UNDER THE AGREEMENTS (UF NOS. 2, 11), DEFENDANTS BREACHED THE TWO AGREEMENTS AND TWO GUARANTEES (UF NOS. 6, 8, 16, 17), AND PLAINTIFF WAS DAMAGED AS A RESULT OF THE BREACHES. (UF NOS. 7, 16.) THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTED TO DEFENDANTS. EVEN ASSUMING THAT DEFENDANTS ARE NOT PRECLUDED FROM RAISING THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES FOR FAILING TO INCLUDE THEM IN THEIR ANSWER, DEFENDANTS STILL HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANTS INTRODUCE NO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF THEIR THEORIES THAT TRINITY IS A DISTINCT LEGAL ENTITY FROM THE BANK OF THE WEST OR THAT TRINITY WAS REQUIRED TO OR FAILED TO FILE A FICTITIOUS NAME STATEMENT. (SEE ROCHLIS V. WALT DISNEY CO. (1993) 19 CAL.APP.4TH 201, 219 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS), HOLDING THAT CLAIMS AND THEORIES NOT SUPPORTED BY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DO NOT RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE.) FURTHER, THE UNAMBIGUOUS LANGUAGE OF THE CONTRACTS STATE THAT TRINITY IS "A DIVISION OF BANK OF THE WEST." (DECLARATION OF JAMES ROACH, EXS. 1, 5 - 7.) ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees is granted. (Civ. Code sec. 1717(a).) Defendants shall pay Plaintiff reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $31,562.00 within 60 days of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Paula Shi #13358.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Or Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar May-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. AND ROBERT HATSUO OZAKI'S Motion To Vacate Or Set Aside Judgment is off calendar per the Withdrawal of Motion filed April 18, 2014. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARC CEFALU, ESQ., 415.438.4600, FOR PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST.
BENJAMIN LOPATIN, ESQ., 415.692.6607, FOR DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. AND ROBERT OZAKI.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs BODY DETAILS, INC. and ROBERT OZAKI's Motion For New Trial is denied. First, the motion does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1113, which requires a memorandum to be filed in support of a motion. Second, the Declaration of Mitchell Sens is inadmissible because it states it was made under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of Florida. In order for a declaration to be admissible, it must be made under the laws of the state of California. (Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 618.) Third, Defendants set forth no basis for setting aside the Court's previous order granting summary judgment in favor of Plaintiff. Defendants cannot establish that there is "newly discovered evidence' which [they] could not, with reasonable diligence, have discovered and produced" earlier. (CCP sec. 657.) Defendants have conducted no discovery since being served with the complaint in August 2012. (Declaration of Marc T. Cefalu, par. 3.) Additionally, Defendants do not identify a specific "accident or surprise" that would require vacating or modifying the Order. (CCP sec. 657.) Lastly, the Court already considered the issue of standing in ruling on the motion for summary judgment and determined "the unambiguous language of the contracts state that Trinity is 'a division of Bank of the West.' (Declaration of James Roach, Exs. 1, 5 - 7.)" (March 7, 2014 Order Granting Summary Judgment.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARC CEFALU, ESQ., 415.438.4600, FOR PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST.
BENJAMIN LOPATIN, ESQ., 415.692.6607, FOR DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. AND ROBERT OZAKI.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs BODY DETAILS, INC. and ROBERT OZAKI's Motion To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment is denied. First, the motion does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1113, which requires a memorandum to be filed in support of a motion. Second, Defendants do not establish any basis for staying the enforcement of judgment, in light of the denial of the motion for new trial. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Vacated

MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Answer And Affirmative Defenses
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP, Tel. 415.438.4600, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANTs BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION and ROBERT OZAKI's MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Answer And Affirmative Defenses is Denied. The motion does not conform to California Rule of Court 3.1324, which provides that a declaration must accompany the motion. Although Defendant submits such a declaration in reply, the Court finds that it would be unduly prejudicial to Plaintiff to consider this evidence, given that Plaintiff does not have an opportunity to respond. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Plaintiff Bank Of The West'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP, Tel. 415.438.4600, for plaintiff and moving party;
Benjamin M. Lopatin, Esq. of the Law Offices of Howard W. Rubenstein, Tel. 800.436.6437, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff met its burden in establishing that Defendant Body Details entered into two commercial lease agreements with Plaintiff, (Undisputed Material Fact ("UF" nos. 1, 10), Defendant Robert Hatsuo Ozaki executed guarantees on the agreements (UF nos. 5, 14), Plaintiffs performed under the agreements (UF nos. 2, 11), Defendants breached the two agreements and two guarantees (UF nos. 6, 8, 16, 17), and Plaintiff was damaged as a result of the breaches. (UF nos. 7, 16.) The burden then shifted to Defendants. Even assuming that Defendants are not precluded from raising their affirmative defenses for failing to include them in their answer, Defendants still have not met their burden of proof. Defendants introduce no evidence in support of their theories that Trinity is a distinct legal entity from the Bank of the West or that Trinity was required to or failed to file a fictitious name statement. (See Rochlis v. Walt Disney Co. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 201, 219 (disapproved on other grounds), holding that claims and theories not supported by admissible evidence do not raise a triable issue.) Further, the unambiguous language of the contracts state that Trinity is "a division of Bank of the West." (Declaration of James Roach, Exs. 1, 5 - 7.) Order and Judgment signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To May-29-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARC T. CEFALU, ESQ., 415.438.4600, FOR PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC. AND ROBERT OZAKI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Lack of personal jurisdiction and inconvenient forum are grounds for separate motions, not a basis for a demurrrer. CCP 418.10(a). In any event, they were not raised at the time of the filing of the first demurrer and are waived. CCP 418.10(e)(3). Further, the forum selection clause is not a basis to sustain the demurrer on the grounds of failure to state a cause of action under CCP 430.10(e). CCP 410.40, cited by Plaintiff, is not a threshold requirement and is inapplicable here. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for claim and delivery is overruled because the Complaint is not uncertain and is not subject to heightened pleading requirements. The demurrer for lack of subject matter jurisdiction is overruled as Defendant has provided no authority to support that California courts have no jurisdiction over contracts such as the one at issue. This is Defendant's second demurrer to the Complaint; Defendant is now directed to file an answer to the Complaint within ten days of entry of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Continued To Mar-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin M. Lopatin, Esq. of the Law Offices of Howard Rubinstein PA, Tel. 800.436.6437, for defendant and moving party;
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. of Cox Wooton Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP, Tel. 415.438.4600, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BODY DETAILS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. Defendants agreed that San Francisco is the appropriate forum for litigating any disputes arising out of the lease. The Court has subject matter jurisidiction. Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Superior Court (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1100 is not on point. Pacific Decision held that a Court exceeds its jurisdiction when it issues a turnover order for property outside the state. The holding in that case was based on strict construction of law pertaining to attachment, and is not applicable to whether the court in this case has subject matter jurisdiction. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
